name: Inventory CSV Import System
scenario: Build a complete inventory file processing system for a Flipkart-style warehouse. Products arrive as CSV files in an input directory. The system validates, processes, archives, and reports — a real-world data pipeline using only Java NIO File API.
description: A file-based inventory importer: reads CSVs from an input directory, validates each row, writes processed records to output, moves valid files to archive, moves problematic files to quarantine, and generates a summary report.

core.description: Directory structure creation: `inventory/input/`, `inventory/archive/`, `inventory/quarantine/`, `inventory/reports/` — all with createDirectories.
Generate 3 sample CSV files in `inventory/input/` with columns: sku, name, quantity, price. At least one file has an invalid row (negative qty or blank name).
`InventoryProcessor.processFile(Path csvFile)` → returns `ProcessingResult` (validCount, errorCount, totalValue). Reads file with Files.readAllLines or Files.lines, skips header, validates each row.
Process all CSV files in input/ using Files.walk or Files.list. Files with 0 errors → move to archive/. Files with any errors → move to quarantine/.
Write `inventory/reports/summary.txt` with: timestamp, per-file results, grand totals (total valid, total errors, total inventory value). Use Files.writeString or Files.write.
Print summary to console AND write to report file.
Proper use of try-with-resources for any Files.lines() or Files.walk() streams.

bonus.description: Add duplicate SKU detection across all files: if the same SKU appears in multiple files, flag it in the report as 'DUPLICATE SKU: [sku]'. Use a ConcurrentHashMap or Set.

Add Files.walk-based cleanup: after processing, delete any files older than 7 days from quarantine/ (simulate with files created more than N seconds ago via Files.getLastModifiedTime). Demonstrate the check even if nothing is actually deleted.
Write per-file detail reports: for each processed CSV, write `inventory/reports/[filename]-detail.txt` with each valid row's computed total (qty × price) and any error rows.

data_to_use:
CSV format: sku,name,quantity,price
File 1 (electronics.csv) — all valid:
SKU-001,Wireless Mouse,45,799
SKU-002,Keyboard,12,1999
SKU-003,USB Hub,60,499

File 2 (accessories.csv) — has errors:
SKU-004,Laptop Stand,25,1299
SKU-005,,8,18999      ← invalid: blank name
SKU-006,Headphones,-5,2999  ← invalid: negative qty

File 3 (books.csv) — all valid:
SKU-007,Java Programming,100,649
SKU-008,Design Patterns,50,899
example_output:
=== Inventory Import System ===

Processing: electronics.csv
  Valid rows: 3, Errors: 0, Value: ₹84042
  → Archived

Processing: accessories.csv
  Valid rows: 1, Errors: 2, Value: ₹32475
  → Quarantined

Processing: books.csv
  Valid rows: 2, Errors: 0, Value: ₹103350
  → Archived

=== Summary ===
Files processed: 3
Archived: 2  |  Quarantined: 1
Total valid rows: 6
Total errors: 2
Total inventory value: ₹219867

Report saved to: inventory/reports/summary.txt

criteria: Reads with readAllLines or lines (10 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Key patterns: Files.list() to iterate input/, try-with-resources for any stream. IOException inside lambda → wrap as UncheckedIOException. Files.move() to archive or quarantine. Files.writeString() for report. ProcessingResult can be a simple record or class.
submission_instructions: Save as InventoryImporter.java. Push to GitHub. This is a real data pipeline pattern.
tips_for_success: Collect files into List<Path> first (don't iterate stream while moving files)
IOException in forEach lambda → catch inside lambda and re-throw as UncheckedIOException
ProcessingResult: record(int valid, int errors, long totalValue)
Move logic: result.errors() == 0 → archive, else → quarantine
Report: Files.write(reportPath, summaryLines) where summaryLines is a List<String>
